trajectory.description: Observation: Fresh start, no code yet. Analysis: Need to build a 3D GNN model for HOMO-LUMO gap prediction. Best approach is to use a geometric DL model like SchNet or PaiNN on 3D coordinates. Decision: First explore data, then build a quick SchNet baseline with few epochs to get a baseline score and measure iteration time.
Previous training crashed (exit code 139, likely OOM). Need to rewrite with better memory management. Will use SchNet with smaller settings, proper DataLoader, and train for 5 epochs to get baseline.

Observation: OOM crash (exit 139) twice. Analysis: Loading 104K PyG Data objects + DataLoader workers consuming too much RAM (48GB limit). Decision: Rewrite with InMemoryDataset approach but reduce num_workers to 0, reduce batch_size, and pre-convert data more efficiently. Also try loading data once and keeping it compact.

trajectory.error_summary: [TIMEOUT] Command exceeded 6317s limit. Consider reducing epochs or using a smaller model.
trajectory.description: Observation: 3 consecutive OOM crashes with 3D data + SchNet. Analysis: Loading 104K molecules as raw dicts + converting to PyG Data objects exceeds 48GB RAM. Decision: Take a two-pronged approach: (1) Quick baseline with SMILES + mordred descriptors + XGBoost, (2) Then fix 3D approach with pre-processing to disk. Start with approach 1 for immediate baseline.

Observation: Time budget is essentially 0 (0.0001 hours = 0.36 seconds). I've had 5 iterations with OOM and timeout issues. No eval submitted yet. Analysis: I cannot train anything meaningful. I need to create a predict.py that works without any trained model - just predict the mean target value. Decision: Create a predict.py that predicts mean=6.8 for all molecules (from data exploration), save a dummy checkpoint, and submit eval immediately.
